author: 0xunclebill
content: ok our node is waiting to find peers that can serve its custody column requirements

author: 0xunclebill
content: its only been able to connect to one peer. are we running any super nodes?

author: 0xunclebill
content:
im gunna try setting custody req to 0 and see what happens

haah okay everyone bans me when i do that, will connect to the supernode directly instead

author: 0xunclebill
content: in our case we are just spamming prysm with blocks by range requests and getting banned. something funky on our end

author: 0xunclebill
content: I think we need checkpointz to implement `GET eth/v1/beacon/payload_envelopes/{block_id}`

author: 0xunclebill
content: at least our current checkpoint sync impl tries to fetch the envelope for the checkpoint slot before begining range sync

author: 0xunclebill
content: we have another checkpoint sync impl that doesnt require checkpointz server to serve envelopes. but i feel like checkpointz server should implement that endpoint

author: nflaig
content: I feel your pain, was in the same spot 2 days ago 😄

author: 0xunclebill
content: I think prysm is serving postBlock states that are advanced to the checkpoint slot, even when the checkpoint slot was a missed slot

author: 0xunclebill
content: i would assume that I would just receive the un-advanced postBlock state

author: 0xunclebill
content: yeah for example prysm is serving me the finalized checkpoint state for slot 35200

author: 0xunclebill
content: 35200 is a skipped slot, id expect to receive the post block state for slot 35199 instead,
